[2020-09-05 06:33] silentslayers: Not counting alternate drones, is P2's 3rd engi deadeyes always worse than the 2 engi version? Coffeeyay says it is always better to do 2 engi deadeyes for P2. I've analyzed along with Sano that for Deadeye + Apollo, p2 likes the 3rd engi to have a cleaner apollo buy. I think there are examples where having more gold early has advantages, so for me its a set to set thing. 

I've heard Mrguy supports coffee, that 2 engi for p2 is always better. But i've heard from other top players, e.g. Yotsuba and I think Msven and definitely others that they think there are times to do the 3rd engi version. Note I'm not including rock paper scissors stuff aspects in this debate, just purely a deadeye mirror situation about having better lines by doing the 3rd engi as P2. Can we get a definitive answer on this? If some top players could weigh in that'd be great!

--- Thread (50 messages, 2020-09-15) ---
[2020-09-15 21:55] anesotericman (Esoteric): since it's a unit the has a lot direct gold+resource cost, a lot of the value depends on how hard it is for the opponent to defend
[2020-09-15 21:56] anesotericman (Esoteric): if it lets them start churning polywalls and plexos, it's pretty bad
[2020-09-15 21:56] anesotericman (Esoteric): if they need to start buying forcefields and engies, it's pretty great
[2020-09-15 21:56] anesotericman (Esoteric): and the effect of this is more pronounced than even other attacks, because it actively trades away future gold for future attack
[2020-09-15 21:58] anesotericman (Esoteric): that said, at a barrier value of 2.4 (which is average of wall+forcefield or EEW defense) it's slightly more efficient than shadowfang without clicking
[2020-09-15 21:59] anesotericman (Esoteric): just keep in mind, that's assuming you don't replace the drones. if you have to buy 3 bad units along with it, it's way, way less good
[2020-09-15 22:00] anesotericman (Esoteric): also keep in mind, that you're only getting a very efficient buy for the ~5 gold worth of resources you put into it. In practice, that can mean you're buying less efficient stuff with the rest of your gold as you're putting a lot of resources into one thing
[2020-09-15 22:01] silentslayers: > also keep in mind, that you're only getting a very efficient buy for the ~5 gold worth of resources you put into it. In practice, that can mean you're buying less efficient stuff with the rest of your gold as you're putting a lot of resources into one thing
Yeah true, that's often why it's not great, but when you *can* buy other good things with it, it's sooo good
[2020-09-15 22:02] silentslayers: > that said, at a barrier value of 2.4 (which is average of wall+forcefield or EEW defense) it's slightly more efficient than shadowfang without clicking
Just wondering, how is this measured?
[2020-09-15 22:03] anesotericman (Esoteric): there's no one right way, but I'm acting as though there's a constant 33.33% interest rate
[2020-09-15 22:03] anesotericman (Esoteric): basically, assuming you can buy drones without energy being relevant
[2020-09-15 22:04] anesotericman (Esoteric): then assuming that the basic tech buildings are just normal efficiency, along with drones
[2020-09-15 22:04] anesotericman (Esoteric): which means gold = 1, red = 1, green = 4/3, blue = 5/3 in value
[2020-09-15 22:05] anesotericman (Esoteric): so wall + forcefield cost = 5B + 1GD = 5 + (5/3) + 1 + (4/3) +3 = 12
[2020-09-15 22:06] anesotericman (Esoteric): there's a lot of assumptions in there, I think other people use 2.3 BV, which seems reasonable as well
[2020-09-15 22:07] anesotericman (Esoteric): so that's to set my assumptions for the costs of resources and interest rate. Everything else is pure math from there.
[2020-09-15 22:10] anesotericman (Esoteric): under these assumptions, 1 damage this turn is worth around 2.771. Threat is worth 1/4 of what a damage is, so around .693. Careful about those, easy to get wrong. A drones is worth 3 (3 * one gold), an attacker is worth 8.314 (3 * one attack)
[2020-09-15 22:12] silentslayers: Where does the 2.771 come from?
[2020-09-15 22:12] anesotericman (Esoteric): 2.4 is the Barrier Value (which is made up)
[2020-09-15 22:12] anesotericman (Esoteric): that's the cost for you defend this very turn of yours
[2020-09-15 22:13] anesotericman (Esoteric): but the opponent needed to defend a half-turn earlier than when you get the attack

messages.author: anesotericman
messages.content: since it's a unit the has a lot direct gold+resource cost, a lot of the value depends on how hard it is for the opponent to defend

messages.author: anesotericman
messages.content: if it lets them start churning polywalls and plexos, it's pretty bad

messages.author: anesotericman
messages.content: if they need to start buying forcefields and engies, it's pretty great

messages.author: anesotericman
messages.content: and the effect of this is more pronounced than even other attacks, because it actively trades away future gold for future attack

messages.author: anesotericman
messages.content: that said, at a barrier value of 2.4 (which is average of wall+forcefield or EEW defense) it's slightly more efficient than shadowfang without clicking

messages.author: anesotericman
messages.content: just keep in mind, that's assuming you don't replace the drones. if you have to buy 3 bad units along with it, it's way, way less good

messages.author: anesotericman
messages.content: also keep in mind, that you're only getting a very efficient buy for the ~5 gold worth of resources you put into it. In practice, that can mean you're buying less efficient stuff with the rest of your gold as you're putting a lot of resources into one thing

Just wondering, how is this measured?

messages.author: anesotericman
messages.content: there's no one right way, but I'm acting as though there's a constant 33.33% interest rate

messages.author: anesotericman
messages.content: basically, assuming you can buy drones without energy being relevant

messages.author: anesotericman
messages.content: then assuming that the basic tech buildings are just normal efficiency, along with drones

messages.author: anesotericman
messages.content: which means gold = 1, red = 1, green = 4/3, blue = 5/3 in value

messages.author: anesotericman
messages.content: so wall + forcefield cost = 5B + 1GD = 5 + (5/3) + 1 + (4/3) +3 = 12

messages.author: anesotericman
messages.content: there's a lot of assumptions in there, I think other people use 2.3 BV, which seems reasonable as well

messages.author: anesotericman
messages.content: so that's to set my assumptions for the costs of resources and interest rate. Everything else is pure math from there.

messages.author: anesotericman
messages.content: under these assumptions, 1 damage this turn is worth around 2.771. Threat is worth 1/4 of what a damage is, so around .693. Careful about those, easy to get wrong. A drones is worth 3 (3 * one gold), an attacker is worth 8.314 (3 * one attack)
